Title: Coordinated regulation of the entry and exit steps of aromatic amino acid biosynthesis supports the dual lignin pathway in grasses

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively decarboxylates arogenate to tyrosine using NADP+ as cofactor.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Aromatic amino acid biosynthesis; tyrosine biosynthesis from arogenate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key regulatory final step in tyrosine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for single-gene transient expression; AtRbcS3B promoter in combinatorial expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing internodes/stems; much lower in leaves; co-expression pattern resembles PTAL
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursors for lignin and other phenylpropanoids in grasses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Alone causes only modest tyrosine increase in Nicotiana, but when co-expressed with feedback-insensitive BdDHS1b strongly enhances tyrosine production while maintaining high phenylalanine accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; recombinant enzyme assay; tyrosine feedback inhibition assay; plastid localization in protoplasts; Agrobacterium transient expression; combinatorial co-expression metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdTyrA1 is strongly expressed in grass internodes where lignification occurs. 2. It is a plastidial NADP+-dependent arogenate dehydrogenase strongly inhibited by tyrosine (IC50 about 66 µM). 3. Alone it has limited effect on tyrosine accumulation in planta, but together with BdDHS1b it synergistically boosts tyrosine while retaining high phenylalanine production.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 13CO2 labeling in growing stems; transient expression in Nicotiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Alone limited by tyrosine feedback inhibition; no strong boost without upstream deregulated DHS1b
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: High Km for arogenate may suit function under high substrate supply from DHS1b-driven flux.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in gene stacking with DHS1b to support simultaneous tyrosine and phenylalanine production for aromatic-product engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively decarboxylates arogenate to tyrosine using NADP+ as cofactor.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Aromatic amino acid biosynthesis; tyrosine biosynthesis from arogenate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key regulatory final step in tyrosine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for single-gene transient expression; AtRbcS3B promoter in combinatorial expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Lower than TyrA1 and TyrAnc; little induction in internodes versus leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Does not significantly increase tyrosine in Nicotiana and does not synergize with BdDHS1b, consistent with tight feedback regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; recombinant enzyme assay; feedback inhibition assay; plastid localization; transient expression and co-expression metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdTyrA2 is a plastidial NADP+-dependent arogenate dehydrogenase. 2. It is the most feedback-sensitive TyrA isoform tested (IC50 about 20 µM tyrosine). 3. Its expression has little in planta effect on tyrosine accumulation and no evident synergy with BdDHS1b.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Tight feedback inhibition limits engineering value for tyrosine elevation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not strongly induced in lignifying internodes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less suitable than TyrAnc or TyrA1+DHS1b stacking for metabolic engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively decarboxylates arogenate to tyrosine using NADP+ as cofactor.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Aromatic amino acid biosynthesis; tyrosine biosynthesis from arogenate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key regulatory final step; deregulated tyrosine-production enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for single-gene transient expression; AtRbcS3B promoter in combinatorial expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced in developing internodes/stems; low in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for dual lignin/phenylpropanoid pathway in grasses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly increases tyrosine in Nicotiana; co-expression with BdDHS1b further boosts tyrosine, but tends to reduce phenylalanine accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogeny; RT-qPCR; recombinant enzyme kinetics; tyrosine inhibition assay; plastid localization; transient expression and co-expression metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdTyrAnc is a plastid-targeted non-canonical TyrA with the highest catalytic efficiency among Brachypodium TyrAs. 2. It has low sensitivity to tyrosine feedback inhibition (IC50 about 242 µM). 3. In planta expression causes strong tyrosine hyperaccumulation, though at the expense of phenylalanine.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong tyrosine increase can lower phenylalanine levels, potentially perturbing phenylalanine-derived metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts similarly to previously described deregulated TyrA enzymes from other lineages but evolved independently.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong tool to elevate tyrosine, but may need balancing/stacking to avoid phenylalanine depletion.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses phosphoenolpyruvate and erythrose 4-phosphate to form 3-deoxy-D-arabino-heptulosonate 7-phosphate (DAHP).
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Shikimate pathway; aromatic amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed across organs and developmental stages
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: AAA precursors supporting lignin and phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Does not significantly alter aromatic amino acid or shikimate levels in Nicotiana, consistent with feedback inhibition.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogeny; in vitro recombinant enzyme kinetics; effector screening; IC50 analysis; transient expression metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdDHS1a is a canonical DHS entry-step enzyme of the shikimate pathway. 2. It is inhibited by arogenate but not strongly by tyrosine, phenylalanine, or tryptophan. 3. Its heterologous expression does not markedly elevate aromatic amino acid pools in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Co-expression pattern less specifically associated with lignifying internodes than DHS1b.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less useful than DHS1b for boosting aromatic amino acid flux.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses phosphoenolpyruvate and erythrose 4-phosphate to form 3-deoxy-D-arabino-heptulosonate 7-phosphate (DAHP).
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Shikimate pathway; aromatic amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key regulatory entry step; naturally deregulated control point
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtRbcS3B promoter; SlRbcS3A promoter in co-expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in internodes; co-expressed with BdPTAL, BdTyrA1 and lignin pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Phenylalanine; tyrosine; tryptophan; shikimate-derived aromatic amino acids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: AAA precursors for lignin and phenylpropanoid production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly boosts shikimate and all aromatic amino acids in planta, especially phenylalanine; synergizes with BdTyrA1 to elevate tyrosine while maintaining high phenylalanine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogeny; expression analysis; recombinant enzyme kinetics; feedback effector screening; transient expression; combinatorial co-expression metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdDHS1b is a grass-specific DHS1 duplicate induced in lignifying internodes. 2. Unlike BdDHS1a and BdDHS2, it is largely insensitive to feedback inhibition by arogenate, tyrosine, phenylalanine, and tryptophan under tested conditions. 3. It dramatically increases aromatic amino acid production in planta and cooperates with BdTyrA1 to support high tyrosine and phenylalanine simultaneously.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May cause broad increases in AAA pools and metabolic rebalancing; especially large phenylalanine accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described by the authors as the first naturally occurring deregulated plant DHS reported.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering tool to push carbon into shikimate/AAA metabolism; especially useful in stacking with TyrA1 or TyrAnc.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses phosphoenolpyruvate and erythrose 4-phosphate to form 3-deoxy-D-arabino-heptulosonate 7-phosphate (DAHP).
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Shikimate pathway; aromatic amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtRbcS3B promoter; SlRbcS3A promoter in co-expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Little effect on aromatic amino acid pools in Nicotiana and no major additive effect with TyrA isoforms, consistent with feedback inhibition by tryptophan and arogenate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogeny; expression analysis; recombinant enzyme kinetics; effector screening; IC50 analysis; transient expression; co-expression metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdDHS2 is a photosynthetic-tissue DHS isoform. 2. It is feedback inhibited by tryptophan and arogenate. 3. It does not substantially boost aromatic amino acid accumulation in heterologous planta assays.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Feedback sensitivity restricts utility for engineering high AAA flux
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely functions as a housekeeping/photosynthetic DHS rather than lignification-associated isoform.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower utility than DHS1b for engineering.
